articles.title: SpaceX suddenly aborts second Starship V3 launch after ignition
articles.description: The company didn't immediately say what went wrong. SpaceX's stock plunged more than 4% in after-hours trading before paring losses.

articles.title: Coca-Cola suspended production at its Fairlife dairy after a ransomware attack
articles.description: Coca Cola said dairy production at its Fairlife unit will "remain suspended" in the United States following a hack.

articles.title: Founders Fund hires former OpenAI exec Ryan Beiermeister (and not because of her ‘Mafia’ skills)
articles.description: Ryan Beiermeister, who demonstrated cool analysis in the Founders Fund YouTube series "Mafia," has joined the firm as a partner.

articles.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
articles.description: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

articles.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
articles.description: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

articles.title: Oil giant BP shutters its corporate venture arm after 20 years
articles.description: BP Ventures is shutting down, ending a nearly 20-year run that was marked by reportedly lackluster returns.

articles.title: Uber’s $14.8B Delivery Hero deal would nearly double its global footprint
articles.description: Uber has agreed to acquire Delivery Hero in a $14.8 billion all-stock deal that would nearly double the company’s global footprint and create one of the world’s largest food-delivery platforms outside China.

articles.title: Newsletter platform Beehiiv now lets subscribers chat with each other, adds AI
articles.description: Beehiiv is launching an AI Copilot to help publishers with user growth and analytics.

articles.title: X cracks down on creators who steal content
articles.description: X will use Grok AI to better detect stolen content, redirect payouts to original creators, and crack down on engagement bait.

articles.title: AI-powered travel agency Fora hits unicorn status, raises $60M
articles.description: Travel agency Fora announced a $60 million Series D round led by Forerunner and Tactile Ventures, valuing the company at $1 billion.

articles.title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
articles.description: With this new update, Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions and into completing tasks across the apps they use regularly.

articles.title: Google continues its renaming streak by turning NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
articles.description: Google said users can soon access their notebooks through AI Mode in Search.

articles.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
articles.description: DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward software designed for AI agents instead of just humans.

articles.title: UK cops say arrest of two young hackers disrupted the operations of an infamous hacking group
articles.description: Owen Flowers and Thalha Jubair, two members of the prolific Scattered Spider hacking group, pleaded guilty and were sentenced to five years and six months in jail for hacking London’s metropolitan transit system.

articles.title: Period tracker Stardust shares users’ health data with analytics firm, says Mozilla research
articles.description: One period tracker app tested by Mozilla was 'squeaky clean,' while another app was seen sharing users' health data with an analytics company, underscoring vast differences in user privacy among these apps.

articles.title: Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball?
articles.description: You may have heard that OpenAI released its first piece of hardware this week. You may not have heard about the ChatGPT basketball.

articles.title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
articles.description: Drawing on more than a decade spent helping build some of the world's most influential AI systems, including research that later informed the development of ChatGPT, Andrew Dai explains why he believes visual AI is one of the next major frontiers in artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Sheryl Sandberg leads $10 million investment in AI-powered vehicle inspection service
articles.description: The startup, founded in 2021, lets enterprise customers use smartphones to scan and spot vehicle damage.

articles.title: Why AMI Labs’ Alexandre LeBrun won’t call his AI ‘AGI’ or ‘superintelligence’
articles.description: While everyone in AI is chasing "superintelligence," Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun’s world model startup, AMI Labs, dismisses the word.

articles.title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
articles.description: The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion.

articles.title: T-Mobile bungled forced plan migration, canceling some users' free lines
articles.description: T-Mobile to restore free lines lost during plan migration, but price hikes remain.

articles.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

articles.title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.
articles.description: Elon Musk's xAI files first lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images.

articles.title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
articles.description: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.

articles.title: Trump teleprompter aide made $100,000 betting on what Trump would say, reports say
articles.description: If only someone could have predicted it.

articles.title: 2026 Toyota RAV4 plug-in: Big battery means daily drives are all-electric
articles.description: Toyota's everyday small SUV should rarely require trips to the gas station.

articles.title: Now, even Russia's most elite hackers are using Clickfix to infect devices
articles.description: The social-engineering technique has primarily been a tool of financially motivated criminals.

articles.title: Linus Torvalds to critics of AI coding in Linux: "Fork it. Or just walk away."
articles.description: Creator says he will "very loudly ignore" those arguing for a ban on AI tools.

articles.title: We've seen helium baked off a rocky exoplanet's atmosphere
articles.description: If the large, rocky planet is losing helium, then we can infer what is left behind.

articles.title: OnePlus confirms shutdown in the US and Europe, ending months of speculation
articles.description: OnePlus promises to continue supporting the phones it has already released.

articles.title: Could China and Russia really destroy Starlink? Only with a boomerang.
articles.description: "We will likely have similar concerns and discussions when China fields its Starlink-like constellation."

articles.title: Energy IPOs surge as investors hunt for ways to play AI boom
articles.description: Companies coming to market are raising money at fastest pace this century.

articles.title: Tesla driver who blamed crash on autopilot pressed accelerator 100%, NTSB finds
articles.description: NTSB findings back Elon Musk's claim that driver manually overrode FSD.

articles.title: Move over, GPS: Navigation satellites in low-Earth orbit are making a comeback
articles.description: Xona aims to deploy 258 satellites into low-Earth orbit as a GPS alternative.

articles.title: Hundreds rally at Bethesda HQ to protest Xbox layoffs, and Ars was there
articles.description: Union wants to halt a "perpetual cycle" of layoffs, get back to contract bargaining.

articles.title: Buzz Aldrin sells famous felt-tip pen that helped launch Apollo from the Moon
articles.description: While an impressive sale, the pen and switch did not break records.

articles.title: Sheetz is quitting VMware, migrating 11,000 virtual machines
articles.description: The convenience store chain will use StorMagic instead.

articles.title: Judge: Trump can’t deport researchers just for working in content moderation
articles.description: Disinformation researchers praise ruling blocking Trump visa denials and deportations.

articles.title: Engineer identifies and explains every '90s computer seen in Jurassic Park
articles.description: Yes, it was, in fact, a Unix system.

articles.title: Windows 0-day drops the same day Microsoft releases record number of patches
articles.description: HiveLegacy is a "powerful primitive" that's likely capable of other nefarious actions.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **SpaceX星舰V3第二次发射在点火后突然中止**，具体原因尚未公布，引发外界对火箭技术稳定性的关注。
2. **可口可乐旗下Fairlife乳制品因勒索软件攻击暂停生产**，显示关键基础设施仍面临严重网络安全威胁。
3. **罗布乐思在移动应用中推出AI驱动的游戏创作功能**，进一步降低用户生成内容的门槛；同时，AI旅行平台Fora获6000万美元融资成为独角兽。
4. **谷歌持续整合AI产品**：推出让用户“出演”AI视频的Google Vids、允许AI模式与特定应用交互，并将NotebookLM更名为Gemini Notebook。
5. **Uber以148亿美元收购Delivery Hero部分业务**，预计将使全球业务版图几乎翻倍；BP在运营20年后关闭其企业风险投资部门。

**最值得关注**：SpaceX星舰发射再次受挫，凸显重型运载火箭技术挑战依然严峻，而AI生成内容与网络安全风险则持续渗透各行各业。

articles.title: How Cars24 scales conversations and builds faster with OpenAI
articles.description: Cars24 uses OpenAI-powered voice and chat agents to handle 1M+ monthly conversation minutes, recover 12% of lost leads, and bring agentic workflows to teams across the company.

articles.title: The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
articles.description: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

articles.title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
articles.description: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.

articles.title: How to manage AI investments in the agentic era
articles.description: Learn how enterprises can manage AI investments in the agentic era by measuring useful work per dollar, improving efficiency, and scaling high-value workflows.

articles.title: How sales teams use ChatGPT Work
articles.description: See how sales teams can use ChatGPT Work to create pipeline briefs, meeting prep packets, forecast reviews, account plans, and stalled-deal diagnoses from real work inputs.

articles.title: How data science teams use ChatGPT Work
articles.description: See how data science teams can use ChatGPT Work to build root-cause briefs, impact readouts, KPI memos, scoped analyses, and dashboard specs from real work inputs.

articles.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
articles.description: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

articles.title: Getting started with ChatGPT
articles.description: Learn how to use ChatGPT, start your first conversation, and discover simple ways to write, brainstorm, and solve problems with AI.

articles.title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
articles.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a majority intend to switch or add providers within the year, many within a quarter. Buying decisions turn on integration and total cost of ownership rather than headline token price — which is fortunate, because most enterprises cannot yet see their unit economics clearly: GPUs sit at half utilizati

articles.title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
articles.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agents, spending remains a thin slice of the security budget, and enterprises are evenly split on whethe

articles.title: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
articles.description: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector databases that define the category — yet a majority of enterprises have already watched their agents produce confident, wrong answers traced to missing or inconsistent context. A governed semantic layer is emerging as the fix, but most are still building it; the field is converging on hybrid retrieval; and even as provider

articles.title: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
articles.description: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated evaluation today; and the most-cited weakness is that evaluations do not align with real-world outcomes. Yet two-thirds already allow, or are actively engineering toward, deploying agent changes to production on automated evaluation alone — with no human in the loop. The result is an evaluation gap —

articles.title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
articles.description: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents” are still chatbot wrappers, the control plane enterprises expect is deliberately hybrid to avoid lock-in, and real-time fiscal control over token burn remains the exception. This wave of VentureBeat Pulse Research examines enterprise agent orchestration: which platforms enterprises run on, what dri

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Embed模型登顶RTEB排行榜**：该模型在检索任务中排名第一，推动了智能体检索技术发展。
2. **谷歌推出多项AI工具更新**：包括扩展搜索应用连接、新增视频创建与编辑功能（Google Vids），并庆祝视觉搜索创新25周年。
3. **OpenAI关注AI安全与青少年权益**：主张青少年应获得安全的AI使用环境，同时披露美国通过州和联邦行动推进AI安全，并发布提升模型鲁棒性的“GPT-Red”自我改进技术。
4. **Hugging Face发布多项技术进展**：包括模型路由复杂性分析、语音AI质量评估工具“Real World VoiceEQ”、PyTorch性能分析教程，以及新项目“Inkling”和“Shippy”智能体构建经验分享。
5. **企业与AI应用案例**：Cars24借助OpenAI扩展对话系统并加速开发；OpenAI披露2026年7月安全事件。

今日最值得关注的进展是NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Embed模型在检索领域取得领先，标志着智能体检索能力的重要突破。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **国际关系与舆论变化**：多国公众对华好感度上升，皮尤研究显示全球更多人倾向于中国而非美国；同时有前美联储顾问因隐瞒与中国关系被判刑，一名美国科学家因研究朝鲜核试验被中方拘留，凸显中美间复杂互动。
2. **科技与安全自主**：多篇文章探讨AI安全与减少对美中依赖，美国诺贝尔奖得主奥马尔·亚吉将移居中国领导AI研究所；另有观点警示中国开源AI可能存在的“陷阱”，反映技术竞争与信任议题。
3. **太空探索进展**：中国“小行星猎手”探测器在飞行十亿公里后抵达目标，标志着深空探测取得新里程碑。
4. **经济与产业动态**：中国经济增速大幅下滑未达目标，同时推进关键矿产外交以保障资源安全；玩具批发采购、短剧娱乐等产业模式受到关注。
5. **社会政策调整**：为鼓励生育，中国开始打击AI恋爱聊天机器人；此外，中俄能否摧毁“星链”系统被讨论，但需依赖“回旋镖”式技术，暗示挑战巨大。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：中国在太空探索取得实质性突破的同时，面临经济增长放缓与科技自主压力，而全球对华态度转暖与中美摩擦并存，凸显中国在复杂国际环境中的机遇与挑战。
